preamble: The transformation from a voiceless aspirated bilabial plosive (pʰ) to a voiced bilabial plosive (b) is a common type of lenition, a sound change that involves the weakening of consonants. This shift typically involves two stages: first, the loss of aspiration (deaspiration), followed by the voicing of the stop. This process can occur in various phonetic environments, often intervocalically or in specific morphological contexts.

examples.note: The Attic Greek voiceless aspirated bilabial plosive /pʰ/ (represented by φ) corresponds to a voiced bilabial plosive /b/ (represented by β) in Ancient Macedonian.
Ancient Macedonian rendered Greek aspirates *ph, th, kh as voiced stops *b, d, g.
